notes: At first, I was getting a BugSplat error at launch, right after the game's splash screen. KF2's error logs were indicating files not found. After much testing, I found out that by installing the "libflvnd-dev" package on my Linux Mint's installation, the problem was solved. It is now running perfectly, but crashes as soon as someone uses voice chat during gameplay. KF2's error logs now state a fatal error following this line: F3DAudioCalculate() Filename not found. Otherwise, great fun!

notes:
I experience occasional crashes on launch. 

Other players using in game VOIP caused a crash every time. 
Workaround: In file SteamApps/compatdata/232090/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/KillingFloor2/KFGame/Config/KFEngine.ini
Change line bHasVoiceEnabled=true to bHasVoiceEnabled=false
Save.
Game on.

title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
The game doesn't launch out of the box, and takes a significant performance hit once tweaked.
For my testing, all in game settings were the ingame default of high - unless otherwise stated.

Wine D3d11 MUST be used in order for the game to load. All game contents will load fine with no anomolies, but the average FPS will be fairly low.

Disabling Esync significantly increased the average FPS, though when in game there's random drops in frame rate. I find the best mitigation for these frame drops is lowering shadow quality - down to medium to remove most frame drops, to low to almost completely remove them

Disabling D3d11 all together causes the game to crash on launch - leaving it unplayable
title: Killing Floor 2

title: Killing Floor 2

notes: Tested with current patch as of 06/14/19 and Proton 4.2-7. While you could play this casually, if you are at all serious or into the hardcore bit of the game, this is unplayable. Inconsistent framerate, micro-stutters, frame drops, hitching, delayed reactions, and atrocious loading for anyone who does not have an SSD. I'll update later after trying a few things, but for now, this is good only for those who want something to play for 30 minutes or an hour on a casual level. Playing it any other way is untenable with its current performance.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
Install GameMode and make a gamemode.ini file from the example on their GitHub. 
 
I changed the startup command line to use more tweaks, this may or may not work depending on your hardware and system so do your homework to know what each parameter is for if you plan to copy/paste it in your Steam. Right click on the game in Steam, click Properties, then click Set Launch Options and use this command line without the quotes:  
 
"__GL_THREADED_OPTIMIZATIONS=1 mesa_glthread=true PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 gamemoderun %command% -nostartupmovies" 

Set game Video option to Ultra preset then disable Vsync and unlock frame rate limit, then lower some settings if needed,  from Ultra settings in my opinion: 
 
lower Shadow to High
lower Ambient Occlusion to SSAO
disable Real Time Reflection 
 
You can see your FPS unlocked this way and adjust, then enable VSync if you have enough FPS or lower more settings.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
You need to add the launch option: PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%, otherwise your CPU clock speed will drop again and again. This would cause an unstable framerate.
Didn't launch on Proton 4.2 with the esync disabled, with Proton 3.7-8 and 3-7.8 Beta it works great.
You need a new Nvidia driver, on 396.54.09 the GPU usage was arround 30% and causing framerates from less then 30fps on my setup. With the Nvidia 430 driver it works great!

title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
Initially I could not get it to run unless WineD3D was enabled in user_settings.py, if I ran defaults (for DXVK), I would get bugsplat error with 0x4069f6a2 (filename not found). The fix to get DXVK running (restore proton version + settings to defaults!) was to install the vulkan-radeon and lib32-vulkan-radeon packages using pacman. This may be Arch specific, and why Ubuntu/other users say it works perfectly OTB meanwhile Arch users say its borked. Full package list of my system at https://pastebin.com/EdqLt0Rt

DXVK perf: 90-100% of Windows performance, FreeSync/VRR works great, mouse sensitivity is sky high for some reason, your Windows sensitivity may not transfer. Found no graphical glitches/T-posing/etc so far in 20 mins of play.

WineD3D perf: ran poorly, with a bug for 1000Hz mice where panning horizontally would judder; the FPS was also 1/3rd of Windows figures. It was ultimately unplayable.
title: Killing Floor 2

notes:
Everything runs out of the box. A little choppiness. If you run the game the first time off a 4k monitor and running in 1080p, the game will try to run in 4k and you will only be able to move the mouse a quarter of the screen. To fix this, edit the ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/232090/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/KillingFloor2/KFGame/Config/KFSystemSettings.ini
----->
Look for ResX and ResY and change them to 1920 and 1080. If using KDE, go to System Settings > Display and Monitor > Compositor. If there is an error message regarding OpenGL, re enable it.
